title: What is the point of V3Coverage?
body:
Title says it all - I am confused on why anyone would use this source file in the first place. From what I have seen running the coverage command in the [_make_tracing_c_](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/tree/master/examples/make_tracing_c) example, at a high verbosity `--debugi 5`, I get the following output: 
```
-- VERILATE ----------------
/home/<home-dir>/verilator -cc --exe -x-assign fast -Wall --trace --assert --coverage -f input.vc top.v sim_main.cpp
- V3Coverage.cpp:268:     Toggle: VAR 0x5564f3c0e460 <e773#> {d14ax} @dt=0x5564f3c19fc0@(G/w1)  clk INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
- V3Coverage.cpp:268:     Toggle: VAR 0x5564f3c0e980 <e781#> {d15ax} @dt=0x5564f3c19fc0@(G/w1)  reset_l INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
- V3Coverage.cpp:268:     Toggle: VAR 0x5564f3c13ce0 <e789#> {d17ax} @dt=0x5564f3c0f090@(G/w2)  out_small OUTPUT [VSTATIC]  WIRE
- V3Coverage.cpp:268:     Toggle: VAR 0x5564f3c14810 <e797#> {d18ax} @dt=0x5564f3c14150@(G/w40)  out_quad OUTPUT [VSTATIC]  WIRE
- V3Coverage.cpp:268:     Toggle: VAR 0x5564f3c18520 <e805#> {d19ax} @dt=0x5564f3c14c80@(G/w70)  out_wide OUTPUT [VSTATIC]  WIRE
- V3Coverage.cpp:268:     Toggle: VAR 0x5564f3c18f10 <e929#> {d20ax} @dt=0x5564f3c0f090@(G/w2)  in_small INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
- V3Coverage.cpp:268:     Toggle: VAR 0x5564f3c19950 <e930#> {d21ax} @dt=0x5564f3c14150@(G/w40)  in_quad INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
- V3Coverage.cpp:268:     Toggle: VAR 0x5564f3c15f80 <e931#> {d22ax} @dt=0x5564f3c14c80@(G/w70)  in_wide INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
- V3Coverage.cpp:385:  IF: IF 0x5564f3c07f80 <e351#> {d38ah}
- V3Coverage.cpp:422:    COVER-branch: IF 0x5564f3c07f80 <e351#> {d38ah}
- V3Coverage.cpp:266:     Disable Toggle: Not relevant signal type VAR 0x5564f3c145d0 <e1012#> {d14ax} @dt=0x5564f3c19fc0@(G/w1)  __Vtogcov__clk MODULETEMP
- V3Coverage.cpp:266:     Disable Toggle: Not relevant signal type VAR 0x5564f3c18cd0 <e1031#> {d15ax} @dt=0x5564f3c19fc0@(G/w1)  __Vtogcov__reset_l MODULETEMP
- V3Coverage.cpp:266:     Disable Toggle: Not relevant signal type VAR 0x5564f3c1a090 <e1050#> {d17ax} @dt=0x5564f3c0f090@(G/w2)  __Vtogcov__out_small MODULETEMP
- V3Coverage.cpp:266:     Disable Toggle: Not relevant signal type VAR 0x5564f3c201e0 <e1181#> {d18ax} @dt=0x5564f3c14150@(G/w40)  __Vtogcov__out_quad MODULETEMP
- V3Coverage.cpp:266:     Disable Toggle: Not relevant signal type VAR 0x5564f3c3d740 <e3592#> {d19ax} @dt=0x5564f3c14c80@(G/w70)  __Vtogcov__out_wide MODULETEMP
- V3Coverage.cpp:266:     Disable Toggle: Not relevant signal type VAR 0x5564f3c6d5d0 <e7803#> {d20ax} @dt=0x5564f3c0f090@(G/w2)  __Vtogcov__in_small MODULETEMP
- V3Coverage.cpp:266:     Disable Toggle: Not relevant signal type VAR 0x5564f3c6ed50 <e7934#> {d21ax} @dt=0x5564f3c14150@(G/w40)  __Vtogcov__in_quad MODULETEMP
- V3Coverage.cpp:266:     Disable Toggle: Not relevant signal type VAR 0x5564f3c8a930 <e10345#> {d22ax} @dt=0x5564f3c14c80@(G/w70)  __Vtogcov__in_wide MODULETEMP
- V3Coverage.cpp:268:     Toggle: VAR 0x5564f3c21620 <e713#> {e10ak} @dt=0x5564f3c19fc0@(G/w1)  clk INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
- V3Coverage.cpp:268:     Toggle: VAR 0x5564f3c21af0 <e721#> {e11ak} @dt=0x5564f3c19fc0@(G/w1)  reset_l INPUT [VSTATIC]  PORT
- V3Coverage.cpp:268:     Toggle: VAR 0x5564f3c228c0 <e950#> {e15ap} @dt=0x5564f3c07d70@(G/w32)  count_c [VSTATIC]  VAR
- V3Coverage.cpp:385:  IF: IF 0x5564f3c25ab0 <e473#> {e17ah}
- V3Coverage.cpp:385:  IF: IF 0x5564f3c259a0 <e472#> {e25ak}
- V3Coverage.cpp:422:    COVER-branch: IF 0x5564f3c259a0 <e472#> {e25ak}
- V3Coverage.cpp:422:    COVER-branch: IF 0x5564f3c25ab0 <e473#> {e17ah}
- V3Coverage.cpp:481:  COVER: COVER 0x5564f3c27b40 <e527#> {e40ae}
- V3Coverage.cpp:266:     Disable Toggle: Not relevant signal type VAR 0x5564f3cbb720 <e14567#> {e10ak} @dt=0x5564f3c19fc0@(G/w1)  __Vtogcov__clk MODULETEMP
- V3Coverage.cpp:266:     Disable Toggle: Not relevant signal type VAR 0x5564f3cbbb50 <e14586#> {e11ak} @dt=0x5564f3c19fc0@(G/w1)  __Vtogcov__reset_l MODULETEMP
- V3Coverage.cpp:266:     Disable Toggle: Not relevant signal type VAR 0x5564f3cbc1a0 <e14605#> {e15ap} @dt=0x5564f3c07d70@(G/w32)  __Vtogcov__count_c MODULETEMP
```

And I am looking for clarifications for the following things:
- What does the address `0x5564f3c0e460` mean? 
- Toggling on and off - what does it signify? From what I have understood [from the code](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/b0e796ca83cd05a6f72674c89e9af1eaa9c71366/src/V3Coverage.cpp#L90), it signifies verilator won't trace the code which has a leading underscore, or exceeds a certain max-width. Then why are wires from the `sub.v` being toggled on and off?
- And finally the miscellanous angle brackets and curly bracket infromation. Is this even needed? 

Thanks for any of the replies :)!
